linear_invariance.verdict: S is invariant under linear growth to machine precision; the framework's LCDM fence holds: w = -1 exactly.

causal.note: two-scale point set: dense 4^3 @20 Mpc/h + sparse 4^3 @1200 Mpc/h

extensive_vs_intensive.note_claim: lambda_maintenance_wz.md sec.3 factorizes S = k_maint * s_bar and argues 'extensive beats intensive'.
extensive_vs_intensive.finding: The factorization assumes s_bar is uniform over links. It is not: links beyond any cosmological horizon carry ~1e-16 of S because C_ij^2 ~ 0 there. Removing whole causal volumes removes links of ~zero relative entropy, so dln(k_maint)/dlna does NOT transfer to dlnS/dlna. The extensive argument fails.

sign_stress_test.note: S(sigma_g^2) strictly decreasing in every trial. The elementwise map c -> (exp(s c)-1)/(exp(s)-1) is a convex-combination of Hadamard powers of c (Schur => PSD) that contracts every off-diagonal toward 0.

desi_matching_cell_scale.note: the cell scale is a FREE CHOICE; this is a calibration, not a prediction

cpl_line.relation: wa = -(1 + w0)  [because w -> -1 as a -> 0: thawing]

general_theorem.statement: For a Gaussian linear field and ANY pointwise transform g, S(C_g) <= S(C_linear); equality iff g is affine. Hence S can only fall below its (constant) linear value and w >= -1 always: no local model of nonlinear growth can produce phantom dark energy.
general_theorem.proof: Mehler/Hermite convex combination + Schur product + Oppenheim + convexity of -logdet
general_theorem.caveat: Gravitational evolution is NOT a pointwise map of the linear field (mass is displaced; the Zel'dovich map is nonlocal). The theorem covers the broad class of LOCAL density transforms, of which the lognormal is the standard analytic member. An N-body measurement of C_ij(a) on fixed comoving cells is the decisive test.

sdss_real_data.reason: Single flux-limited low-z sample. The lever arm in ln a is 0.12, over which the predicted change in S is <2%. Splitting into z-shells makes luminosity-dependent galaxy bias b(z,L) evolve with the shell, and b drops out of the NORMALIZED correlation matrix only if it is scale- and epoch-independent -- exactly the assumption that fails in a flux-limited sample. The bias systematic is degenerate with the signal. No usable time evolution.
